what_it_does: Tracks completion criteria, verification status, decisions, deviations, follow-ups, and iteration notes for finishing a resolution-core implementation and then a mobile gallery/viewer upgrade campaign.
observations: The file begins as a truth table for a finish run, with GREEN tied to verification by use rather than code reading.
It records binding disciplines such as derive-never-place, system-not-use, openness/extensibility, grounded naming, and honest deviation reporting.
Phase 1 lists RC-A through RC-D as completed resolution-core criteria covering typed relationship edges, role-based resolving, dial targeting, and derived build geometry.
Phase 2 tracks a mobile major upgrade with criteria M1 through M-close, followed by a reopened form-review sequence driven by Tim's visual feedback.
Each mobile entry mixes requirement text, implementation notes, browser/device verification claims, desktop stability checks, proof counts, screenshots, and backlog flags.
The document repeatedly distinguishes completed mechanism work from deferred tuning, Tim-gated decisions, and visual polish follow-ons.
The shown excerpt is a running campaign ledger as much as a completion-criteria document.
standouts: The file treats verification as experiential and operational: proofs, server smoke, browser use, screenshots, device-width checks, scrollbar census, and desktop pixel stability.
It preserves reversals and corrections in place, including dark-shell default changes, reopened mobile form review, and over-claimed device confirmation.
It frames UI behavior through declared mechanisms such as slots, fills, dials, controls-as-records, split panes, marks, polarity, and reusable pinch zoom.
conventions: Uses criterion IDs and tags such as RC-A, rcore-3, M4, mob-12, and mob-21.
Uses FUNCTION and FORM subsections to separate behavioral and visual/structural verification.
Uses checked boxes, warning flags, DONE markers, HONEST GAP/DEVIATION notes, and BACKLOG references.
Uses dated Tim decisions and quoted feedback as steering records.
Keeps desktop stability and mobile redesign verification separate during the mobile phase.
Breaks a strict criteria-only format by embedding long chronological implementation journal entries.
concerns: The file is very long and accumulative, making current status hard to extract without reading many historical notes.
Some completed sections retain unchecked subcriteria from the original wording, which can make status ambiguous from this file alone.
The shown content is truncated, so the final state of the mob-21 entry and anything after it is not visible here.
Several entries mix requirements, proof logs, design rationale, known gaps, and backlog capture in one paragraph, reducing scanability.
notes: This is the campaign ledger for resolution-core completion and the mobile gallery/viewer upgrade, with status tied to use-based verification and visible design review.
questions: What is the final visible status after the truncated mob-21 entry?
Are BACKLOG items maintained separately as structured records or only referenced from this narrative?
Which open Tim-gated decisions remain active at the end of the full file?
Is this document intended to remain a historical journal, or should completed criteria be condensed after closure?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches: it defines completion criteria and records verification toward completion, while also serving as a detailed iteration journal and decision log.
apparent_themes: derive values from declarations rather than placement
mobile visual hierarchy and low text
honest tracking of gaps and deviations
declared slots, fills, roles, dials, and controls
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to keep implementation, design intent, verification evidence, user decisions, and deferred work aligned during a high-friction finish campaign.
novelty: It uses a completion-criteria document as a combined truth table, proof ledger, design critique log, decision record, and reusable-mechanism audit.
contribution: This file contributes a file-local campaign model for keeping implementation criteria, verification evidence, design principles, user feedback, mechanism generalization, and unresolved follow-ups together in one auditable ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A dated completion-criteria and status ledger for the counterpart-design resolution core and mobile gallery/viewer upgrade, recording criteria, implementation outcomes, verification-by-use evidence, Tim feedback, deviations, and backlog flags.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to steer and audit the finish run so resolution behavior and mobile UI changes are derived, verified in use, visually reviewed, and honestly tracked when incomplete or decision-gated.
